goal: Make /app/contacts and /app/contacts/demo-contact-1 read like one connected relationship workspace instead of separate inspection pages, while preserving all existing local, side-effect-free behavior.

success_criteria.description: GET /app/contacts first viewport leads with a current relationship review queue: who needs attention now, why that person matters, the source context, and the next safe action before search controls or diagnostics.

Each visible contact row offers a readable path into the person detail view and shows source, status, relationship value, and next action as user-facing relationship work rather than technical inventory.

GET /app/contacts/demo-contact-1 first viewport carries the selected person forward with source story, relationship stage, priority reason, and prepare-follow-up action; raw evidence IDs are not dominant primary copy.

Action previews and route recovery states show no outside account, message, notification, contact write, search index read, or database query occurs; list/detail empty, pending, and failure states keep meaningful recovery controls and reloading the same action route remains stable.

Page tests assert the queue-to-detail narrative, visible detail links, action safety ledger, route-state recovery, and copy guardrails; npm test and npm run build pass with no feature, service, API, fixture, shared UI, shell, or unrelated route edits.

out_of_scope: Changing feature contracts, services, service factories, API envelopes, route handlers, or sample data fixtures
Changing global app shell, global CSS, shared UI primitives, profile, events, follow-ups, chat, dashboard, agent, acquisition, or home routes
Adding live address book, email, calendar, CRM, notification, messaging, storage, search, AI, or network integrations
